PARAM.description: inside/outside BSP tree representing the new region
PARAM.name: newTree
PARAM.content: inside/outside BSP tree representing the new region

RETURN.description: a new region, copy of the instance
RETURN.content: new region, copy of the instance

RETURN.description: true if the instance is empty
RETURN.content: true if the instance is empty

PARAM.description: root node of the sub-tree (must have {@link Region Region} tree semantics, i.e. the leaf nodes must have {@code Boolean} attributes representing an inside/outside property)
PARAM.name: node
PARAM.content: root node of the sub-tree (must have {@link Region Region} tree semantics, i.e. the leaf nodes must have {@code Boolean} attributes representing an inside/outside property)

RETURN.description: true if the sub-tree starting at the given node is empty
RETURN.content: true if the sub-tree starting at the given node is empty

RETURN.description: true if the instance covers the full space
RETURN.content: true if the instance covers the full space

PARAM.description: root node of the sub-tree (must have {@link Region Region} tree semantics, i.e. the leaf nodes must have {@code Boolean} attributes representing an inside/outside property)
PARAM.name: node
PARAM.content: root node of the sub-tree (must have {@link Region Region} tree semantics, i.e. the leaf nodes must have {@code Boolean} attributes representing an inside/outside property)

RETURN.description: true if the sub-tree starting at the given node covers the full space
RETURN.content: true if the sub-tree starting at the given node covers the full space

PARAM.description: region to check against the instance
PARAM.name: region
PARAM.content: region to check against the instance

RETURN.description: true if the instance contains the specified tree
RETURN.content: true if the instance contains the specified tree

RETURN.description: a code representing the point status: either Location#INSIDE, Location#OUTSIDE or Location#BOUNDARY
RETURN.content: a code representing the point status: either {@link Location#INSIDE}, {@link Location#OUTSIDE} or {@link Location#BOUNDARY}

RETURN.description: projection of the point on the boundary
RETURN.content: projection of the point on the boundary

PARAM.description: true if boundary attributes are included
PARAM.name: includeBoundaryAttributes
PARAM.content: if true, the boundary attributes at internal nodes are guaranteed to be included (they may be included even if the argument is false, if they have already been computed due to a previous call)

RETURN.description: BSP tree with boundary attributes if includeBoundaryAttributes is true
the size of the boundary (this is 0 in 1D, a length in 2D, an area in 3D ...)
RETURN.content: the size of the boundary (this is 0 in 1D, a length in 2D, an area in 3D ...)

RETURN.description: the size of the instance (this is a length in 1D, an area in 2D, a volume in 3D ...)
RETURN.content: the size of the instance (this is a length in 1D, an area in 2D, a volume in 3D ...)

RETURN.description: one of Side.PLUS, Side.MINUS, Side.BOTH or Side.HYPER (the latter result can occur only if the tree contains only one cut hyperplane)
RETURN.content: one of {@link Side#PLUS Side.PLUS}, {@link Side#MINUS Side.MINUS}, {@link Side#BOTH Side.BOTH} or {@link Side#HYPER Side.HYPER} (the latter result can occur only if the tree contains only one cut hyperplane)
